title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox struggled against the Astros, starting the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst since 2019.
Boston's pitching, a key focus for improvement, has underperformed with a combined 5.22 ERA in six games.
Troubling defensive issues persist, with the team committing six errors in their first six games of the season.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs's trial for strangulation and assault charges is set for May 4 in Dedham District Court.
The charges involve an alleged altercation with his personal chef over owed money that occurred on December 2.
Diggs has pleaded not guilty, maintaining the allegations are due to an unresolved financial dispute with the chef.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Sergeant Scott Quigley allegedly drove drunk and caused a fatal crash in 2023, killing Angelo Schettino.
State Police failed to notify the Middlesex DA’s office about the crash, delaying disclosure of Quigley’s alleged intoxication.
Middlesex DA Marian Ryan's office conducted an internal investigation, revealing gaps in communication and the mishandling of incident details.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, sentenced to 15 to 20 years for fatally stabbing Jazreanna Sheppard in July 2023.
The altercation stemmed from an online feud; the women had never met in person prior to the incident.
Partsch previously pleaded guilty to assault charges in 2017, receiving a two-year prison sentence.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston and rebrand as the Houston Comets, a historic WNBA team.
NBA star Kevin Durant expressed optimism for Connecticut fans, highlighting the success of UConn women's basketball.
Steve Pagliuca's $325 million offer to bring the Sun to Boston was blocked, favoring Houston's $300 million bid.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora scratched Carlos Narvaez from Wednesday's lineup against the Astros, opting for Connor Wong instead.
Cora declined to comment on whether Narvaez's removal was disciplinary, stating it should remain between him and Narvaez.
Narvaez confirmed his lineup change was not injury-related and expressed respect for Cora's decision.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel confirms the team plans to exercise Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option for $18.1 million.
Gonzalez's 2023 rookie contract was extended to 2027; negotiations for a longer extension loom ahead of free agency.
Despite early injuries, Gonzalez has been a standout, earning Pro Bowl and All-Pro honors in recent seasons.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm has scored seven points in his last six games for the Boston Bruins.
Lindholm was moved to a third-line role post-Olympics but has improved with five goals recently.
Boston Bruins won four straight games, and David Pastrnak achieved a career-best 66 assists this season.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson, 33, from Everett, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson's participation is inspired by the 2013 bombing and the city's resilient response to the tragedy.
She aims to honor the trauma teams whose lifesaving work reflects Boston's spirit of skill and compassion.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit, primarily due to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
Mayor Wu's administration has delayed hiring and frozen spending to address the deficit, affecting food, travel, and supplies.
Boston Public Schools also faces a separate $53 million deficit, planning cuts and a partial hiring freeze to manage finances.
